# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure MuleSoft Inference Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Create a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Add Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Configure the Trigger For the Flow

A trigger (source) starts the flow when specified conditions are met. If a connector has its own
associated sources, you can use a connector-specific trigger to initiate the flow. Each connector
trigger has a unique configuration.

You can configure the following sources for this connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener trigger, follow these steps:

1.  Click the **Listener** component on the canvas UI.
2.  (Optional) Click the edit icon to change the name of the **Listener** component.
3.  (Optional) If you created a configuration file for the connector, select it.
4.  In the **General** tab, in the **Path** field, enter the relative path for the path set in the
    HTTP configuration element. See
    [Configure Paths](../../http-connector/latest/http-listener-ref#configure-paths).
5.  Select the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Allowed methods</p></td><td><p>To allow all HTTP methods (default), do not define this attribute. To restrict certain methods, specify all allowed HTTP methods, for example, <code>GET, POST</code>, as a comma-separated list.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response streaming mode</p></td><td><p>Whether to use streaming when the response is sent.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>Appends a key-value pair for a reader property to the value of the Output MIME attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary node only</p></td><td><p>Select this option to execute the <strong>Listener</strong> trigger on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery policy</p></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../../http-connector/latest/http-documentation#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Response</p></td><td><p>HTTP error response returns a status code for the error.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>Body of the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Headers to include in the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status code</p></td><td><p>Status code of the error response, for example, <code>500</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason phrase</p></td><td><p>Reason for the error, for example, <code>Gateway Timeout</code> or <code>Internal Server Error</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add the Connector to Your Integration Project

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.

4.  Click the
    ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
    (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

    ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

5.  Enter `mulesoft inference` in the search box.
6.  Select the connector to add it to your project.

Adding a connector to an integration project does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Add a Global Configuration

1.  In the configuration XML, place your cursor before the `<flow/>` element and type
    `ms-inference`.

    Ensure that the cursor is not inside the `<flow/>` element.

2.  Select the snippet. Here are some examples (using OpenAI) based on the available configurations:

    ```xml
    <ms-inference:text-generation-config name="OpenAITextGen" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Text generation config" doc:id="1ef66d01-d3ec-442a-bf61-99919d73f97e" >
    <ms-inference:openai-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.llmModel}" timeout="60000" />
    </ms-inference:text-generation-config>
    ```

    ```xml
    <ms-inference:moderation-config name="OpenAIModeration" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Moderation config" doc:id="8bd9c1fe-3176-4319-9d91-090e4e6da3cc" >
        <ms-inference:openai-moderation-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.moderation}"/>
    </ms-inference:moderation-config>
    ```

    ```xml
    <ms-inference:image-generation-config name="OpenAIImageGen" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Image generation config" doc:id="aba51427-1dd3-4915-a411-8fe6543ee6ac" >
        <ms-inference:openai-image-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.imageGenModel}" />
    </ms-inference:image-generation-config>
    ```

    ```xml
    <ms-inference:vision-config name="OpenAIVision" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Vision config" doc:id="797661b2-a4ac-493d-8550-45d05d03da17" >
        <ms-inference:openai-vision-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.visionModel}" />
    </ms-inference:vision-config>
    ```

3.  Add the attributes to complete the configuration.

## Configure Provider-Specific Settings

Based on your chosen inference provider, you may need to configure additional settings. For example:

```xml
<ms-inference:text-generation-config
    provider="OPENAI"
    modelName="gpt-4o"
    temperature="0.7"
    maxTokens="1000"
    timeout="60"
    name="OpenAIConfig">
</ms-inference:text-generation-config>
```

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for the connector, configure the other required fields. The
required fields vary depending on which connector actions you use.

For more information about configuring MuleSoft Inference Connector actions, see
[MuleSoft Inference Connector - Additional Configuration Information](mulesoft-inference-additional-config).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [MuleSoft Inference Connector Reference](mulesoft-inference-connector-reference)
- [MuleSoft Inference - Additional Configuration Information](mulesoft-inference-additional-config)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# MuleSoft Inference Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These examples show you how to use MuleSoft Inference Connector with the OpenAI inference type. To
use other inference types, you must change these configurations.

## Example: Text Generation

This Mule app showcases the use of three distinct text generation operations to interact with
OpenAI. Each operation is demonstrated in its own dedicated flow.

- **\[Agent\] Define Prompt Template**

  This operation is used to create structured prompts for AI agents. In this example, itâs
  configured to enable an agent to analyze customer feedback and determine appropriate actions based
  on sentiment, leveraging the configured OpenAI LLM.

- **\[Chat\] Answer Prompt**

  This operation provides simple, direct question-answering capabilities. The example demonstrates
  its use by asking the LLM a straightforward factual question: `What is the capital of Germany?`.

- **\[Chat\] Completions**

  This operation facilitates multi-turn, context-aware conversations. The example illustrates this
  by providing the LLM with an initial conversational context and then asking it to continue the
  dialogue by asking `What is the capital of Switzerland?`.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ms-inference="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference/current/mule-ms-inference.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="1d867172-d19a-4ab1-b194-52c2ad455d7f" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<ms-inference:text-generation-config name="OpenAI_Text_generation_config" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Text generation config" doc:id="3c24c806-bb9f-4512-bc9b-a51cda5c710a" >
		<ms-inference:openai-connection openAIModelName="${config.llmModel}" apiKey="${config.apiKey}" timeout="60000" >
		</ms-inference:openai-connection>
	</ms-inference:text-generation-config>

	<flow name="AgentDefinePromptTemplateFlow" doc:id="1ee32ee4-8c15-4636-9a49-0784f782cb98" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="8725c446-5f45-4732-8fca-ea9006aabed6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/prompttemplate"/>
		<set-variable value='#[%dw 2.0
			output application/json
			---
			{
			"template": "You are a customer satisfaction agent, who analyses the customer feedback in the dataset. Answer via json output and add a type for the result only with positive or negative as well as the complete answer",
			"instructions":"If the customer feedback in the dataset is negative, open a service satisfaction case and apologize to the customer. If the customer feedback in the dataset is positive, thank the customer and wish them a nice day. Do not repeat the feedback and be more direct starting the conversation with formal greetings",
			"dataset": "The training last week was amazing, we learned so much and the trainer was very friendly"
		}]' doc:name="Set Variable" doc:id="209a7846-c3e3-4a8a-9c63-6ab02a772ec4" variableName="testPayload"/>
		<ms-inference:agent-define-prompt-template doc:name="[Agent] Define Prompt Template" doc:id="699b48f4-0ac8-46b5-9f3b-418070e7157d" config-ref="OpenAI_Text_generation_config">
			<ms-inference:template ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.template]]]></ms-inference:template>
			<ms-inference:instructions ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.instructions]]]></ms-inference:instructions>
			<ms-inference:data ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.dataset]]]></ms-inference:data>
		</ms-inference:agent-define-prompt-template>
	</flow>

	<flow name="ChatAnswerPromptFlow" doc:id="698ff181-7968-4294-8f08-51387fbf1dfa" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="3ae26012-7b44-41ee-8d6d-bdb51ed9e39e" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/chatprompt"/>
		<set-variable value='#[%dw 2.0
			output application/json
			---
			{
			    "prompt": "What is the capital of Germany?"
			}]' doc:name="Set Variable" doc:id="774dde86-5241-4151-b7fa-b5d7bf412508" variableName="testPayload"/>
		<ms-inference:chat-answer-prompt doc:name="[CHAT] Answer Prompt" doc:id="53e77f54-95c1-42da-b447-b5703212e9ca" config-ref="OpenAI_Text_generation_config">
			<ms-inference:prompt ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.prompt]]]></ms-inference:prompt>
		</ms-inference:chat-answer-prompt>
	</flow>

	<flow name="ChatCompletionsFlow" doc:id="ae0b651f-950d-40d0-8c98-9ef050779c06" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="a2a68fe6-47da-41b0-954f-3cf51e80d6f2" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/chatcompletion"/>
		<set-variable value='#[%dw 2.0
			output application/json
			---
			[{
			  "role": "assistant",
			  "content": "You are a helpful assistant."
			},
			{
			  "role": "user",
			  "content": "What is the capital of Switzerland!"
			}
			]]' doc:name="Set Variable" doc:id="860b37bd-9fa9-4584-90d4-2723b2e00cc5" variableName="testPayload"/>
		<ms-inference:chat-completions doc:name="[Chat] Completions" doc:id="f2eedc4f-a87b-4579-92a6-4022db9f3c03" config-ref="OpenAI_Text_generation_config">
			<ms-inference:messages ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload]]]></ms-inference:messages>
		</ms-inference:chat-completions>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Example: Image Generation

This Mule app showcases the use of the **\[Image\] Generate** operation to interact with OpenAI.
This operation is used to generate images based on a prompt. In this example, itâs configured to
generate an image of a penguin dancing on the beach being happy with ice cream.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ms-inference="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference/current/mule-ms-inference.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="1d867172-d19a-4ab1-b194-52c2ad455d7f" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<ms-inference:image-generation-config name="OpenAIImageGenConfig" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Image generation config" doc:id="813d08c8-7e6d-4c9e-8259-b80a84093016" >
		<ms-inference:openai-image-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.imageGenModel}" />
	</ms-inference:image-generation-config>

	<flow name="GenerateImageFlow" doc:id="698ff181-7968-4294-8f08-51387fbf1dfa" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="3ae26012-7b44-41ee-8d6d-bdb51ed9e39e" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/generateimage"/>
		<set-variable value='#[%dw 2.0
		   output application/json
		    ---
			{
				"prompt" : "Generate a picture of a penguin dancing on the beach being happy with an ice cream."
			}]' doc:name="Set Variable" doc:id="55ffb06c-d91b-4419-8517-a2d83802413a" variableName="testPayload"/>
		<ms-inference:generate-image doc:name="[Image] Generate (only Base64)" doc:id="9c849d82-b443-4253-bdbd-1fad73df7a45" config-ref="OpenAIImageGenConfig">
			<ms-inference:prompt ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.prompt]]]></ms-inference:prompt>
		</ms-inference:generate-image>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Example: Toxicity Detection

This Mule app showcases the use of the **\[Toxicity\] Detection by Text** operation to interact with
OpenAI. This operation is used to detect harmful content in a text. In this example, itâs configured
to detect harmful content in the text `You are fat`.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ms-inference="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference/current/mule-ms-inference.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="1d867172-d19a-4ab1-b194-52c2ad455d7f" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<ms-inference:moderation-config name="OpenAIInvalidApiKeyModerationConfig" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Moderation config" doc:id="c28800c5-75d8-4904-8e98-a6b8d965e054" >
		<ms-inference:openai-moderation-connection apiKey="${openai.apiKey}" openAIModelName="${openai.llmModel}"/>
	</ms-inference:moderation-config>

	<flow name="ToxicityDetectionFlow" doc:id="1ee32ee4-8c15-4636-9a49-0784f782cb98" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="8725c446-5f45-4732-8fca-ea9006aabed6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/toxicitydetection"/>
		<set-variable value='#[%dw 2.0
		   output application/json
		    ---
			{
    			"prompt": "You are fat"
   			}]' doc:name="Set Variable" doc:id="7ced3e9a-a3ec-4768-85b1-081849fca0fc" variableName="testPayload"/>
		<ms-inference:toxicity-detection-text doc:name="[Toxicity] Detection by Text" doc:id="cd45c2f9-cb7f-4fe0-8311-2a58cc6c11ac" config-ref="OpenAIInvalidApiKeyModerationConfig">
			<ms-inference:text ><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.prompt]]]></ms-inference:text>
		</ms-inference:toxicity-detection-text>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Example: Tools

This Mule app showcases the use of the **\[Tools\] Native Template (Reasoning only)** operation to
interact with OpenAI. This operation is used to create autonomous agents that can use external tools
whenever a prompt canât be answered directly by the AI model. This operation provides a request only
to execute the tools provided in the payload. It doesnât execute them. In this example, itâs
configured to use the tools to get the current temperature in Zurich in Celsius.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ms-inference="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ms-inference/current/mule-ms-inference.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="1d867172-d19a-4ab1-b194-52c2ad455d7f" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<ms-inference:text-generation-config name="OpenAI_Text_generation_config" doc:name="MuleSoft Inference Text generation config" doc:id="3c24c806-bb9f-4512-bc9b-a51cda5c710a" >
		<ms-inference:openai-connection openAIModelName="${config.llmModel}" apiKey="${config.apiKey}" timeout="60000" >
		</ms-inference:openai-connection>
	</ms-inference:text-generation-config>

	<flow name="ToolsNativeTemplateFlow" doc:id="1ee32ee4-8c15-4636-9a49-0784f782cb98" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="8725c446-5f45-4732-8fca-ea9006aabed6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/toolstemplate"/>
		<set-variable
				value="#[%dw 2.0
            output application/json
            ---
            {
                'template': 'You are an helpful assistant',
                'instructions': 'Answer the request with politeness.',
                'dataset': 'What is the current temperature in Zurich in Celsius?',
                'tools': [
                    {
                        'type': 'function',
                        'function': {
                            'name': 'get_current_temperature',
                            'description': 'Get the current temperature for a specific location',
                            'parameters': {
                                'type': 'object',
                                'properties': {
                                    'location': {
                                        'type': 'string',
                                        'description': 'The city and state, e.g., San Francisco, CA'
                                    },
                                    'unit': {
                                        'type': 'string',
                                        'enum': ['Celsius', 'Fahrenheit'],
                                        'description': 'The temperature unit to use. Infer this from the user\'s location.'
                                    }
                                },
                                'required': ['location', 'unit']
                            }
                        }
                    },
                    {
                        'type': 'function',
                        'function': {
                            'name': 'get_rain_probability',
                            'description': 'Get the probability of rain for a specific location',
                            'parameters': {
                                'type': 'object',
                                'properties': {
                                    'location': {
                                        'type': 'string',
                                        'description': 'The city and state, e.g., San Francisco, CA'
                                    }
                                },
                                'required': ['location']
                            }
                        }
                    },
                    {
                        'type': 'function',
                        'function': {
                            'name': 'get_delivery_date',
                            'description': 'Get the delivery date for a customer\'s order. Call this whenever you need to know the delivery date, for example when a customer asks \'Where is my package\'',
                            'parameters': {
                                'type': 'object',
                                'properties': {
                                    'order_id': {
                                        'type': 'string',
                                        'description': 'The customer\'s order ID.'
                                    }
                                },
                                'required': ['order_id']
                            }
                        }
                    }
                ]
            }]"
				doc:name="Set Variable"
				doc:id="9b80bd71-922a-4739-804b-29ac2ba7ef7a"
				variableName="testPayload" />
		<ms-inference:tools-native-template
				doc:name="[Tools] Native Template (Reasoning only)"
				doc:id="e87a2e5e-b19b-4c2d-a4a0-8e991291de97"
				config-ref="OpenAI_Text_generation_config">
			<ms-inference:template><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.template]]]></ms-inference:template>
			<ms-inference:instructions><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.instructions]]]></ms-inference:instructions>
			<ms-inference:data><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.dataset]]]></ms-inference:data>
			<ms-inference:tools><![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.tools]]]></ms-inference:tools>
		</ms-inference:tools-native-template>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Example: MCP Tools

This Mule application showcases the use of the **\[MCP\] Tooling** operation to interact with OpenAI
via MCP Connector. This operation enables you to execute tools hosted on an MCP server directly
through user-defined instructions. In this example, the operation uses the tools to check the
inventory status for a specific part number, `MULETEST0`.

```xml
<flow name="TestNewInferenceFlow2">
	<http:listener path="path" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="zecmdx" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" />
	<set-variable doc:id="7edeecec-4d47-4bfe-a699-23987b60ac14" doc:name="Set Variable" value="#[%dw 2.0
			output application/java
			---
			{
				'template': 'You are an helpful assistant',
				'instructions':'Answer the request with politeness.',
				'dataset': 'Check Inventory for MULETEST0'

			}]" variableName="testPayload" />
	<ms-inference:mcp-tools-native-template doc:name="[MCP] Tooling" doc:id="6783c672-bc72-49c7-bf3a-3f3379e3c9a9" config-ref="Text-generation-config">
		<ms-inference:mcp-config-references>
			<ms-inference:mcp-config mcpClientConfigRef="MCP_Client_SSE_ERP" />
			<ms-inference:mcp-config mcpClientConfigRef="MCP_Client_HTTP" />
		</ms-inference:mcp-config-references>
		<ms-inference:template>
			<![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.template]]]>
		</ms-inference:template>
		<ms-inference:instructions>
			<![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.instructions]]]>
		</ms-inference:instructions>
		<ms-inference:data>
			<![CDATA[#[vars.testPayload.dataset]]]>
		</ms-inference:data>
	</ms-inference:mcp-tools-native-template>
	<ee:transform doc:id="8eda33bd-db2f-412a-b448-dc633d326a39" doc:name="Transform Message">
		<ee:message>
			<ee:set-payload>
				<![CDATA[%dw 2.0
                output application/json
                ---
                {
                    payload: payload,
                    attributes: attributes
                }]]>
			</ee:set-payload>
		</ee:message>
	</ee:transform>
</flow>

<!-- MCP client configs -->
<mcp:client-config name="MCP_Client_SSE_ERP" doc:name="MCP Client" doc:id="fd3bb75d-82e1-4aed-a1e3-f7c7ca6e28fb" clientName="MCP_Client_SSE_ERP" clientVersion="1.0.0">
        <mcp:sse-client-connection requestTimeout="60" serverUrl="${mcp.erpServer}" />
 </mcp:client-config>


<mcp:client-config name="MCP_Client_HTTP" doc:name="MCP Client" doc:id="163c2f4f-86bd-4860-b3b1-5606259f324f" clientName="MCP_Client_HTTP" clientVersion="1.0.0">
        <mcp:streamable-http-client-connection serverUrl="${mcp.httpServer}">
            <mcp:default-request-headers>
                <mcp:default-request-header key="client_id" value="${mcp.httpServer.clientId}" />
                <mcp:default-request-header key="client_secret" value="${mcp.httpServer.clientSecret}" />
            </mcp:default-request-headers>
        </mcp:streamable-http-client-connection>
    </mcp:client-config>
```

## Example: Additional Request Attributes

This feature showcases the ability to control the formatting of LLM outputs directly from the
connector using the **Additional Request Attributes** parameter. With this parameter, you can now
specify a JSON object with attributes that are flattened into the root level of the request payload.
In this example, you can directly pass a `response_format` with type `json_schema` followed by the
corresponding JSON schema. This is included in the request sent to the Inference API, enabling
consistent and structured output in the provided JSON schema.

```xml
<ms-inference:chat-answer-prompt doc:name="[Chat] Answer Prompt" doc:id="fe114415-0f76-4f8f-9bd0-968f1cf2c3e5" config-ref="MuleSoft_Inference_Text_generation_confi_OpenAI">
	<ms-inference:prompt ><![CDATA[What is the capital of India? give country and city in a json format]]></ms-inference:prompt>

	<ms-inference:additional-request-attributes ><![CDATA[{
		  "response_format": {
		    "type": "json_schema",
		    "json_schema": {
		      "name": "location_schema",
		      "schema": {
		        "type": "object",
		        "properties": {
		          "countryName": {
		            "description": "The name of the country.",
		            "type": "string"
		          },
		          "city": {
		            "description": "The name of the city.",
		            "type": "string"
		          }
		        },
		        "required": [
		          "countryName",
		          "city"
		        ]
		      }
		    }
		  }
		}]]>
	</ms-inference:additional-request-attributes>

</ms-inference:chat-answer-prompt>
```

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Inference - Additional Configuration Information](mulesoft-inference-additional-config)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure MuleSoft Inference Connector](mulesoft-inference-connector-studio)
- [MuleSoft Inference Connector Reference](mulesoft-inference-connector-reference)
---

# MuleSoft Inference Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft Inference Connector provides operations to interface directly with the API of various
inference providers, enabling seamless integration of AI capabilities into your Mule applications.

## Configurations

---

### Image Generation Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#ImageGenerationConfig_HerokuImage">Heroku AI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageGenerationConfig_HuggingFaceImage">Hugging Face</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageGenerationConfig_OpenaiImage">OpenAI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageGenerationConfig_StabilityAiImage">Stability AI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ImageGenerationConfig_XaiImage">xAI</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures an expiration policy for the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Heroku AI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Heroku Inference Model</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Heroku Diffusion URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Heroku diffusion URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://us.inference.heroku.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Hugging Face

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hugging Face Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [\[Image\] Generate (only Base64)](#GenerateImage)

### Moderation Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. #### Connection Types

##### Mistral AI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mistral AI Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenAI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open AI Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [\[Toxicity\] Detection by Text](#ToxicityDetectionText)

---

### Text Generation Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a Foundry</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_AzureOpenai">Azure OpenAI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_Cerebras">Cerebras</a></p></li><li><p><a Studio</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_Mistralai">Mistral AI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_Nvidia">NVIDIA</a></p></li><li><p><a Endpoint</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_Openrouter">OpenRouter</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#TextGenerationConfig_Perplexity">Perplexity</a></p></li><li><p><a configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures an expiration policy for the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### AI21Labs

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ai21 Labs Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Anthropic

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anthropic Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Azure AI Foundry

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Azure Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure AI Foundry] Resource Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Azure AI Foundry resource name.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure AI Foundry] API Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Azure AI Foundry API version.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Azure OpenAI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Azure Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure OpenAI] API Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Azure OpenAI API version.</p></td><td><p>2024-10-21</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure OpenAI] User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help to monitor and detect abuse.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure OpenAI] Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The complete endpoint for the Azure OpenAI service. You can either provide the default or a custom endpoint URL (which is used directly and overrides any parameter values), or provide the resource name and deployment ID (which are used to construct the base URL).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure OpenAI] Resource Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resource name of the Azure OpenAI service used to construct the endpoint. This parameter is used if you do not provide a custom endpoint URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure OpenAI] Deployment ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Deployment ID of the Azure OpenAI service used to construct the endpoint. This parameter is used if you do not provide a custom endpoint URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Cohere

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cohere Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Databricks

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Databricks] Serving Endpoint Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Databricks serving endpoint name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Databricks] Serving Endpoint URL host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Databricks serving endpoint URL host.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### DeepInfra

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deep Infra Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Deepseek

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deepseek Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Docker

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Docker Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Docker Models] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Docker models base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:12434</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token.
