##### GitHub

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Git Hub Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### GPT4All

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Gpt4all Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[GPT4ALL] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>GPT4ALL base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:4891/v1</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Groq

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Groq Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Heroku AI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Heroku Inference Model</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td><p><code>claude-3-7-sonnet</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Heroku Inference URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Inference URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://us.inference.heroku.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Hugging Face

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hugging Face Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. ##### NVIDIA

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>NVIDIA Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model Name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>NVIDIA Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>NVIDIA base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://integrate.api.nvidia.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Ollama

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ollama Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Ollama] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ollama base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:11434/api</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenAI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open AI Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OpenAI model name.</p></td><td><p><code>gpt-4o-mini</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenAI Compatible Endpoint

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open AI Compatible Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OpenAI compatible model name.</p></td><td><p><code>gpt-4o-mini</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[OpenAI Compatible] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OpenAI compatible base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://server.endpoint.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenRouter

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open Router Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

###### Parameters

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. ##### Swisscom

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Swisscom Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td><p><code>meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Swisscom] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Swisscom base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.swisscom.com/layer/swiss-ai-platform/{modelName}/v1</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Together

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Together Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. ## Supported Operations

- [\[Agent\] Define Prompt Template](#AgentDefinePromptTemplate)
- [\[Chat\] Answer Prompt](#ChatAnswerPrompt)
- [\[Chat\] Completions](#ChatCompletions)
- [\[MCP\] Tooling](#McpToolsNativeTemplate)
- [\[Tools\] Native Template (Reasoning only)](#ToolsNativeTemplate)

---

### Vision Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_AnthropicVision">Anthropic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_AzureAiFoundryVision">Azure AI Foundry</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_GeminiVision">Gemini</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_GithubVision">GitHub</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_GroqVision">Groq</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_HuggingFaceVision">Hugging Face</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_MistralaiVision">Mistral AI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_OllamaVision">Ollama</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_OpenaiVision">OpenAI</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_OpenrouterVision">OpenRouter</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_PortkeyVision">Portkey</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_VertexaiExpressVision">Vertex AI Express</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#VisionConfig_XaiVision">xAI</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures an expiration policy for the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Anthropic

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anthropic Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Azure AI Foundry

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Azure AI Foundry Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure AI Foundry] Resource Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Azure AI Foundry resource name.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Azure AI Foundry] API Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Azure AI Foundry API version.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Gemini

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Gemini Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Ollama

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ollama Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>[Ollama] Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ollama base URL.</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:11434/api</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenAI

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open AI Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OpenRouter

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#NtlmProxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open Router Model Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Model name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, then you must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key as required by the inference provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers are additional HTTP headers to include with each request. For any existing header key, the custom value will take precedence and overwrite the existing one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout value set for each inference HTTP request.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout unit for the <strong>Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Tokens</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the number of LLM tokens to use when generating a response. This field helps control the usage and costs when engaging with LLMs.</p></td><td><p>500</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Temperature</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number between 0 and 2. The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The temperature is used to control the randomness of the output. When you set it higher, youâll get more random outputs. When you set it lower (towards 0), the values are more deterministic.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top P</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Controls diversity by creating a nucleus of the most probable words to choose from for the next token. Specifies the cumulative probability score threshold that the tokens must reach.</p></td><td><p>0.9</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [\[Image\] Read by (Url or Base64)](#ReadImage)

## Operations

## \[Image\] Generate (only Base64)

`<ms-inference:generate-image>`

This operation generates image based on the text prompt using AI image generation models.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs text prompt describing the desired image.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Request Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional attributes in the request payload to generate more relevant and precise LLM outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ImageResponseAttributes">Image Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Image Generation Config](#ImageGenerationConfig)

### Throws

- MS-INFERENCE:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-INFERENCE:IMAGE_GENERATION_FAILURE
- MS-INFERENCE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MS-INFERENCE:RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- MS-INFERENCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## \[Toxicity\] Detection by Text

`<ms-inference:toxicity-detection-text>`

This operation detects harmful content in a text.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Text to moderate. Can be a single string or an array of strings.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Request Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional attributes in the request payload to generate more relevant and precise LLM outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Moderation Config](#ModerationConfig)

### Throws

- MS-INFERENCE:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-INFERENCE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MS-INFERENCE:RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- MS-INFERENCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-INFERENCE:TOXICITY_DETECTION_OPERATION_FAILURE

## \[Agent\] Define Prompt Template

`<ms-inference:agent-define-prompt-template>`

This operation defines a prompt template with instructions and data.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Template string.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Instructions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Instructions for the LLM.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Primary data content.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional Request Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional attributes in the request payload to generate more relevant and precise LLM outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Text Generation Config](#TextGenerationConfig)

### Throws

- MS-INFERENCE:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-INFERENCE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MS-INFERENCE:INVALID_PROVIDER
- MS-INFERENCE:MCP_SERVER_ERROR
- MS-INFERENCE:MCP_TOOLS_OPERATION_FAILURE

- MS-INFERENCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-INFERENCE:TOOLS_OPERATION_FAILURE

`<ms-inference:chat-completions>`

(conversation history).

This operation executes MCP tools using a defined template that includes instructions, data, and
tools fetched directly from MCP servers.
